title: BIS Quarterly Review, March 2026 - Bank for International Settlements
summary: The BIS Quarterly Review March 2026 compiles research on international banking and financial market developments, including analysis of synthetic risk transfer (SRT) markets, sovereign bond market dynamics, AI infrastructure financing, private credit, leveraged retail investor behavior, and offshore financial activity. The publication contains dedicated sections directly relevant to market structure, particularly the analysis of SRT risks from a system-wide perspective, offshore activity patterns, and private credit market structure.
key_points: Table of contents indicates a dedicated article on 'The rise and risks of synthetic risk transfers' covering SRT economics, market overview, growth potential, and system-wide risks
AI infrastructure financing analyzed through both on- and off-balance-sheet borrowing mechanisms
Private credit's software lending sector examined in context of AI disruption
Analysis of leveraged retail investor behavior in silver and gold markets
Sovereign bond markets discussion covers divergence amid monetary and fiscal policy outlook changes
Research on offshore activity through the lens of BIS statistics, including reallocation of lending and investing patterns
BISTRO tool introduced as a general purpose oracle for macroeconomic time series forecasting
why_it_matters: The BIS Quarterly Review provides authoritative research on global financial system structure and risks. The SRT analysis is particularly relevant for understanding how credit risk is being transferred and concentrated across the financial system, which has direct implications for market structure, counterparty risk, and systemic vulnerability assessments. The publication also covers offshore financial activity patterns and the role of non-bank financial intermediaries.
macro_context: The publication addresses macro-financial linkages including monetary policy communication under uncertainty, monetary policy transmission in emerging markets with different financial vulnerabilities, and how external shocks affect policy responses across economies.
market_structure_context: Direct relevance to market structure includes: SRT market analysis (credit risk transfer mechanisms), offshore financial activity patterns, private credit market structure, sovereign bond market dynamics, and the role of non-bank financial intermediaries in system-wide risk allocation.
